vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely accurate but requires adjustment for current physical status. P1 (Yellow Pot) contains String of Nickels. P2/P4 (Black Pot, shared) contains Mexican Mint and Silver Guest. P3 (Black Pot) contains Pothos and the White Rabbit anchor.

inventory_reconciliation.Systemic_Loss: None identified, though P3 shows significant senescence.

plant_audit.P1: String of Nickels: Stable, showing consistent foliage density.
plant_audit.P2: Mexican Mint: Stable, foliage remains consistent.
plant_audit.P3: Pothos: High stress. One leaf is fully necrotic/yellowed; the other remains turgid but shows signs of chlorosis.
plant_audit.P4: Silver Guest: Stable, integrated within the P2 pot environment.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Consistent moisture levels observed across all pots; no signs of surface cracking or fungal bloom.
biome_observations.biome_anomalies: Debris (white particulate/perlite) is present on the surface of P3, likely from potting medium disturbance.

temporal_deltas.T-5_to_T-3: Minimal change; P3 leaf yellowing is already established.
temporal_deltas.T-2_to_T-1: Lighting variance makes assessment difficult, but structural integrity of the P3 leaf remains constant.
temporal_deltas.CURRENT: No significant morphological changes compared to T-1; the necrotic leaf in P3 remains attached.

vision_report.anomalies: The P3 Pothos specimen is exhibiting advanced senescence in one leaf, likely due to transplant shock or nutrient deficiency.
vision_report.narrative_description: The biome is currently stable, with the exception of the P3 Pothos. The yellowing leaf in P3 is a clear indicator of physiological stress, likely related to the transition to the current indoor environment. The other specimens (P1, P2, P4) appear to be acclimating well to the fixed LED/North window light regime. No new growth or pests were detected.
